research-topics.title: Complex Event Processing
research-topics.description: Data is generated at high speed today, from sensor networks, stock markets, smart cities, and social networks, among others. These data sources are called data streams. Complex Event Recognition (CER) is a class of data management technologies that model data streams as sequences of events, where users are interested in recognizing complex events, namely, groups of events on a stream that represent critical situations in real life. In this line of work, we study the foundations of CER systems, starting with the query language for specifying patterns of complex events, continuing with the computational model for internally representing these patterns, and concluding with optimizing and efficiently evaluating these models.

research-topics.title: Information Extraction
research-topics.description: Most data today is semi-structured, like JSON files, CSV files, or plain text files, and extracting and transforming data from them is a repetitive task in data-intensive workloads. To speed up this task, rule-based information extraction allows one to specify patterns, like regex, for the data pieces one wants to extract and transform. In this line of research, my group studies the foundations of rule-based information extraction, enhancing classical regex with novel extraction mechanisms, usually referred to in the area as document spanners. We study extensions of the classical theory of regular expressions and automata theory, and efficient algorithms for evaluating them, to enable a useful, efficient process for extracting and transforming data from text documents.

important-work.author: Alejandro Grez, Cristian Riveros, Martín Ugarte, Stijn Vansummeren

research-topics.title: Automata Theory
research-topics.description: Automata theory studies state-based computational models for computing patterns or functions over sequences. In my group, we usually apply automata theory to data management tasks on data structured as sequences (like text documents, streams, or paths in graph data). But we also studied the foundations of automata theory, exploring new models and properties, and proposing new algorithms for automata-related problems.

research-topics.title: Query Evaluation
research-topics.description: Relational databases are probably the most popular systems for storing data structured as tables, and querying data by using SQL. In this line of research, my group studies efficient storing of relation data and processing of SQL queries.

description: People and digital systems generates today data at high speed like sensors networks, stock markets, smart cities, or social networks, among others. These data sources are usually referred to as data streams: continuous sequences of data generated at high speed and without end. Complex Event Recognition (CER) is a class of technologies that model data streams as sequences of events, where users are interested in recognizing complex events, namely, groups of events on a stream that represent critical situations in real life. Examples of complex events could include a fire detected by a sensor network in a nature reserve, an accident recognized by cameras in a smart city, or a critical social event in a social network. To detect these complex event, a complex event recognition engine is a data management software that must efficiently process such data and alert on the presence of complex events in real time.

In this line of work, my group study the foundations of complex event recognition systems starting from the query language to specify patterns of complex events, continue with the computational model to internally represent such patterns, and the optimization and efficient evaluation of such models.
